data.name: StatusScriptCapped
data.value: Each SELECT in the script was limited to the first {0} rows — turn off “Page query results” in Settings to run them unbounded.

data.name: ConfirmKillSession
data.value: Kill session {0}? This closes its connection.

data.name: ConfirmCancelQuery
data.value: Cancel the running query on session {0}?

data.name: ConfirmDropLogin
data.value: Drop login {0}? This cannot be undone.

data.name: McpDesc
data.value: Let an AI client (Claude Desktop/Code) query your connections over MCP while SQL Explorer is open. Only connections you opt in (per-connection AI access) are ever visible.

data.name: McpBindHint
data.value: The server always binds to 127.0.0.1 (this machine only) — it is never reachable from the network.

data.name: McpNoAuthWarning
data.value: ⚠️ Without authentication, any local program on this device can run queries on your AI-accessible connections.

data.name: McpTokenHint
data.value: Put this in your AI client's config. Don't add it to a shared repo or backup. Regenerating invalidates the old token immediately.

data.name: McpScrubSecretsHint
data.value: Scans result values for tokens, keys and secret-named columns and replaces them before the AI sees them. A safety net, not a guarantee.

data.name: McpNoScrubWarning
data.value: Redaction is off — live secrets in query results (tokens, API keys, passwords) are sent to the AI verbatim.

data.name: McpAllowConnectionCreateHint
data.value: When on, an AI client can create database connections over MCP. Off by default. New connections are grouped under the folder below; only loopback hosts are allowed unless you add more.

data.name: McpConnectionCreateWarning
data.value: Any local process that can reach the MCP server can now create connections — and run DDL against a temporary loopback one. Keep this off unless you need it.

data.name: McpAllowedHostsHint
data.value: Hosts an AI-created connection may target. Loopback (localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1) is always allowed.

data.name: McpAllowedHostInvalid
data.value: That doesn't look like a valid host or IP address.

data.name: AiAccessHint
data.value: None = invisible to the AI. Read-only = SELECT/EXPLAIN. Read-write = also INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (DDL is never allowed over MCP).

data.name: AiWriteWarning
data.value: ⚠️ Read-write lets the AI modify data on this connection. Only enable it on a connection you're sure about — never on production unless you really mean to.

data.name: ManagedConnectionWarning
data.value: This connection is managed by “{0}”. Changing its provider, host or port here can break the link to the container it belongs to — the plugin created and maintains it.

data.name: ExcludeFromMcp
data.value: Never expose to the AI (hard block)

data.name: ExcludeFromMcpHint
data.value: Blocks this connection from MCP entirely, even if AI access is set — a safety override for sensitive/production connections.

data.name: DefinitionUnavailable
data.value: No definition is available for this object.

data.name: RoutineExecuteHint
data.value: Enter values for the input parameters. A call script opens in a new tab — press Run to execute it.

data.name: EmptyHint
data.value: Double-click a table to browse, or start a new query.

data.name: ConnectionFailed
data.value: Could not connect to the server.

data.name: ReconnectMessage
data.value: The connection is still open with its previous settings. Your changes only take effect after reconnecting. Reconnect now?

data.name: ChangesOnReconnect
data.value: Changes saved — they take effect the next time you connect.

data.name: ImportConnectionStringHint
data.value: Paste a connection string to fill the fields

data.name: ImportDone
data.value: Fields filled from the connection string.

data.name: ImportNotSupported
data.value: This provider cannot import a connection string.

data.name: SearchObjects
data.value: Search tables, views, columns, or run a command

data.name: AutoIncrementHint
data.value: Auto-increment (identity). Rendered per engine: Postgres GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY, MySQL AUTO_INCREMENT, SQL Server IDENTITY(1,1), SQLite INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT.

data.name: PrimaryKeyHint
data.value: Primary key. Uniquely identifies each row; multiple checked columns form a composite key.

data.name: SingleBottomPanel
data.value: Show one bottom panel at a time

data.name: SingleBottomPanelHint
data.value: Opening a bottom panel (Output, Containers…) closes the others. Turn off to keep several open at once.

data.name: ConfirmBeforeSave
data.value: Show a review dialog before saving changes

data.name: QueryTimeoutHint
data.value: Cancel a running query after this many seconds. 0 disables the timeout. Applies to queries run from the app; the MCP server has its own.

data.name: BrowsePageSizeHint
data.value: Number of rows fetched per page when browsing a table. Applies to newly opened browse tabs.

data.name: QueryPageSizeHint
data.value: When on, a single SELECT with no TOP/LIMIT of its own is shown one page at a time (use Previous/Next to walk the rows), so a stray "SELECT * FROM big_table" doesn't pull everything at once. A multi-statement script can't have a page bar, so each such SELECT in one is limited to a single page of rows instead, and the result tab says so. Queries with their own TOP/LIMIT and non-SELECT statements run unchanged. Applies to newly opened query tabs.

data.name: CloseToTray
data.value: Close to system tray instead of quitting

data.name: CloseToTrayHint
data.value: The close button hides the window and keeps the app running in the background (e.g. the MCP server). Quit from the tray icon or File > Exit.

data.name: AllowMultipleInstancesHint
data.value: When off, launching the app again brings the running window to the front. When on, each launch opens its own independent window. Takes effect on the next launch.

data.name: QueryLogDesc
data.value: Record executed queries to an on-disk audit log you can browse, filter and export. Separate from the always-on query history.

data.name: QueryLogHint
data.value: Only the SQL and timing/outcome are stored — never result rows or connection secrets. The log rotates automatically at its size limit.

data.name: QueryLogPolicyOff
data.value: Query logging is off — enable it in Settings → Query Log to record queries.

data.name: QueryLogPolicyAppOnly
data.value: Only application queries are being logged — AI/MCP logging is off (Settings → Query Log).

data.name: QueryLogPolicyMcpOnly
data.value: Only AI/MCP queries are being logged — application logging is off (Settings → Query Log).

data.name: MasterPwSectionDesc
data.value: Optionally protect your saved connection passwords with an app master password, on top of the OS keychain.

data.name: MasterPwNotSet
data.value: No master password is set. Your connection secrets are protected only by the OS keychain.

data.name: MasterPwNoRecovery
data.value: There is no recovery: if you forget the master password, saved secrets cannot be decrypted and connections must be re-entered.

data.name: MasterPwEnabledMsg
data.value: Master password enabled — connection secrets re-encrypted.

data.name: MasterPwUnlockDesc
data.value: Enter your master password to unlock your saved connections.

data.name: MasterPwSetDesc
data.value: Choose a master password. It encrypts your saved connection passwords. There is no recovery if you forget it.

data.name: MasterPwChangeDesc
data.value: Enter your current and a new master password. All connection secrets will be re-encrypted.

data.name: MasterPwMismatch
data.value: The new passwords do not match.

data.name: ExitConfirmMessage
data.value: Are you sure you want to quit SQL Explorer?

data.name: RestoreTabsOnStartup
data.value: Reopen the previous session's query tabs

data.name: PromptSaveQueryOnClose
data.value: Ask to save query files with unsaved changes on close

data.name: ShowSystemDatabases
data.value: Show system databases (applies on next refresh)

data.name: AboutTagline
data.value: A cross-platform, multilingual SQL explorer built in .NET. Database drivers ship as plugins.

data.name: AboutCopyright
data.value: © 2026 Lionear · Licensed under the Commons Clause License Condition v1.0

data.name: FolderDeleteNote
data.value: Deleting a non-empty folder moves every connection under it (including sub-folders) to "ungrouped" — connections are never deleted. Renaming a folder rewrites the path prefix of every connection beneath it.

data.name: ManagerEmptyHint
data.value: Select a connection or folder, or add a new one.

data.name: DeleteFolderConfirm
data.value: Move {0} connection(s) to ungrouped and delete this folder?

data.name: AppearanceDesc
data.value: Theme applies live — no restart needed.

data.name: PluginsDesc
data.value: Only plugins that declare their own settings appear here. Installing or enabling plugins happens in the Plugin Store.

data.name: PluginsNoneHint
data.value: No installed plugin declares settings yet. Plugins expose their own settings through the SDK.

data.name: ToolDestructiveConfirm
data.value: This action modifies the database and cannot be undone. Continue?

data.name: ConfirmDeleteConnection
data.value: Delete the connection "{0}"? This cannot be undone.

data.name: KeyboardDesc
data.value: Click a shortcut, then press the key combination you want. Changes apply live — no restart.

data.name: ShortcutConflictBanner
data.value: Two commands share a shortcut — resolve the conflict before saving.

data.name: ConfirmDiscardChanges
data.value: This tab has unsaved changes. Close it and discard them?

data.name: ConfirmDiscardChangesMany
data.value: {0} tabs have unsaved changes. Close them and discard the changes?

data.name: StoreRestartNeeded
data.value: Some changes take effect after a restart.

data.name: StoreDiscoveryDesc
data.value: Automatically provided by Lionear; not editable.

data.name: StoreConsentBody
data.value: This plugin requests the following capabilities:

data.name: Cap_storage_Desc
data.value: Keeps its own data and settings in a private file.

data.name: Cap_connections_Desc
data.value: Reads your connections and can add its own managed ones — never your passwords.

data.name: Cap_panel_Desc
data.value: Adds a panel to the workspace, next to Output and History.

data.name: Cap_menu_Desc
data.value: Adds items to the Tools menu and to a connection's right-click menu.

data.name: Cap_background_Desc
data.value: Runs a background task while the app is open.

data.name: Cap_process_Desc
data.value: Can start external programs on your machine (e.g. docker).

data.name: Cap_services_Desc
data.value: Registers the plugin's own services in the app so it can wire them up. It cannot replace or read the app's own services.

data.name: Cap_providers_Desc
data.value: Reads which database engines are installed and how to containerise them. Read-only — it cannot change or control your connections.

data.name: StoreIncompatOld
data.value: Built for host API {0}, no longer supported (minimum {1}).

data.name: StoreIncompatMin
data.value: Requires host API {0}+; this build is {1}.

data.name: StoreManualHint
data.value: For stores not (yet) in Discovery — private, internal, or intentionally unregistered. Fully self-managed. Plugins from a source run inside the app with access to your saved connections, so only add sources you trust. https only.

data.name: StoreSourceInsecure
data.value: Store sources must be an https URL (http is only allowed for localhost). Plugin code is run in-process, so it is not fetched over an unprotected connection.

data.name: MainPluginRestartBanner
data.value: Plugin changes are pending — restart to apply them.

data.name: UpdateBannerRefetched
data.value: Newer build {0} downloaded — install and restart now?

data.name: UpdateBannerUpToDate
data.value: You're already on the latest build.

data.name: UpdateDialogGuided
data.value: Downloaded — drag SQL Explorer to Applications, then reopen it.

data.name: UpdateRollback
data.value: Roll back to the previous version

data.name: SettingsUpdatesHint
data.value: Choose which builds SQL Explorer checks for. You can switch anytime.

data.name: UpdateChannelPreviewDesc
data.value: What's merged into main, ahead of the next Stable.

data.name: UpdateChannelNightlyDesc
data.value: Nightly build. Newest features, may be rough.

data.name: UpdateLogUpToDate
data.value: Checked for app updates ({0}): up to date.

data.name: UpdateLogDismissed
data.value: Checked for app updates ({0}): {1} available (dismissed earlier).

data.name: UpdateLogFailed
data.value: Checked for app updates ({0}): check failed (offline?).

data.name: PluginUpdateLogFailed
data.value: Checked for plugin updates: check failed (offline?).

data.name: PluginUpdateHeldBackHint
data.value: · update SQL Explorer to unlock them

data.name: PluginUpdateLogHeldBack
data.value: Checked for plugin updates: {0} held back (need a newer app).

data.name: PluginUpdateLogAutoStaged
data.value: Auto-staged {0} plugin update(s) — applied on restart.

data.name: PluginUpdatesTooltip
data.value: Compatible plugin updates are available — open the Plugin Store

data.name: OpenQueryNoConnection
data.value: Connect to a database before opening a query file.
